provost.name: Andrew P. Daire (through May 1, 2026); Tina L. Freiburger (Interim, effective May 2026)

ai_strategy_lead.name: Scott Genung and Purush Papatla
ai_strategy_lead.title: Co-leads, UWM AI Taskforce (CIO; Northwestern Mutual Data Science Institute co-director)

documents.title: AI at UWM (central hub for strategy, policy, services)
UWM AI Policies & Guidance (policy analysis of applicable UW System and UWM rules)

ai_strategy.summary: UWM has not published a single named AI strategy document, but has stood up a formal posture through its AI Taskforce (formed spring 2024, ~50 stakeholders in six work groups), GenAI Advisory Group (chaired by CIO Scott Genung with the Provost, SVC Finance and VC Student Affairs as executive sponsors), a central uwm.edu/ai hub, and an Applied AI program anchored by the Connected Systems Institute and the Northwestern Mutual Data Science Institute. The UWM approach emphasizes responsible-use guardrails mapped to UW System information security and data classification policies, a three-tier tool portfolio (Equitable/Targeted/Special) featuring Microsoft Copilot as the default enterprise tool, and heavy alignment with the systemwide AI strategy articulated by UW System President Jay Rothman. UWM participates in a February 2026 five-university Milwaukee AI pact hosted by Northwestern Mutual.

key_provisions: Use of generative AI must comply with UW System Regent Policy Document 25-3 (Acceptable Use), 25-5 (Information Security), UWSA Policy 1031 (Data Classification), 1039 (Risk Management), and 1040 (Privacy)

Confidential/restricted data (student records, research, financial info) forbidden from public AI platforms; Low Risk data approved for on-prem, private-cloud, and public-cloud; Medium Risk requires review by infosec and research computing; High Risk requires additional PII review; research data requires IRB plus security review

Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat (enterprise) is the only version approved for use with University data, protected by the UWM-Microsoft enterprise data protection agreement and identifiable by the green shield indicator
Faculty strongly encouraged (not required) to include a stated AI-use policy in course syllabi; CASL (formerly CETL) publishes sample language
AI detection software discouraged; use of third-party AI detectors may violate FERPA when student work is uploaded to external services
Unauthorized or uncited use of AI tools like ChatGPT is academic misconduct under Wisconsin Administrative Code Chapter UWS 14, investigated through the Dean of Students office
Developers must disclose AI components and explain decisions; users must review outputs for accuracy, bias, and ethical concerns before relying on them
ai_policy.summary: UWM's AI policy posture is cautious and layered, relying primarily on adopted UW System Board of Regents and UWSA policies (1031, 1039, 1040, RPD 25-3, RPD 25-5) rather than a standalone UWM policy. The AI Taskforce and GenAI Advisory Group produced a data risk classification framework (low/moderate/high) tied to permitted AI tools, with Microsoft Copilot Chat as the approved enterprise channel for University data. Syllabus-level AI policy is strongly encouraged but not mandated.

bodies.name: UWM AI Taskforce
bodies.scope: ~50 campus stakeholders in six work groups: Education & Curriculum (Sarah Riforgiate), Student Experience (Dave Clark), Research (Kris O'Connor), Business Processes (Amanda Obermeyer), Infrastructure and Data (Beth Schaefer), Responsible AI (Thomas Malaby). Identified ~60 current/proposed GenAI applications, designed a three-tier tool portfolio, and recommended pilot tools (Microsoft Copilot for Enterprises, Ida, ChatGPT for EDU, Maizy, Azure AI/OpenAI).

bodies.name: GenAI Advisory Group
bodies.scope: Chaired by CIO Scott Genung; executive sponsors Provost Andrew Daire, SVC Finance and Administration Robin Van Harpen, and VC Student Affairs Kelly Haag. 13 members spanning IT, learning technology, faculty, enrollment, HR, business services, data science, infosec, research computing, CETL/CASL, and innovation partnerships. Charged with mapping use cases, establishing governance, communicating risks, and potentially forming a UWM Center of Excellence for GenAI.

bodies.name: Information Security Office (UWM IT)
bodies.scope: Reviews Medium/High Risk AI use cases per UW System data classification; gates research-computing AI integrations; operates infosec@uwm.edu review channel.

bodies.name: Dean of Students Office
bodies.scope: Investigates AI-related academic misconduct under UWS 14; publishes instructor guidance discouraging AI detectors.

governance_structure.summary: UWM's AI governance is a two-body structure: a broad cross-campus AI Taskforce (spring 2024, six work groups, ~50 members) that produced the initial use-case inventory and tool portfolio, and a smaller executive GenAI Advisory Group chaired by the CIO with Provost/SVC-Finance/VC-Student-Affairs sponsorship that is now operationalizing policy, risk, and tool adoption. The Information Security Office and Dean of Students provide functional governance on data risk review and academic misconduct.

initiatives.title: Microsoft AI Co-Innovation Lab at UWM (manufacturing focus)
initiatives.description: Microsoft's first AI Co-Innovation Lab dedicated to manufacturing, and its first co-innovation lab based at a university, housed within the UWM Connected Systems Institute. Partners Microsoft, UWM, Wisconsin Economic Development Corporation, and TitletownTech; companies participate for free, retain IP, and work with small teams to build custom AI solutions (real-time fault detection, multilingual voice assistants). Roughly 10 companies completed the initial cohort.
initiatives.significance: A flagship industry-academic lab for applied AI in manufacturing that gives UWM a national role in Industry 4.0 and embeds undergraduates/graduates and faculty in live AI deployments.

investment.funding_source: Microsoft Corporation (broader southeastern Wisconsin AI infrastructure investment encompassing the lab)
investment.description: Part of Microsoft's $3.3B AI infrastructure and digital transformation investment in Wisconsin. The Co-Innovation Lab specifically is an operational commitment rather than a fixed-dollar grant to UWM.

collaborations.description: Four-way partnership with Microsoft AI Co-Innovation Labs leadership (Ricardo Villarreal, Julianna Javor) and UWM (Chancellor Mark Mone at launch, Chief Innovation and Partnership Officer Brian Thompson, CSI Director Joe Hamann).

tools_and_vendors.name: Azure AI / Azure OpenAI
tools_and_vendors.role: Underlying AI platform for lab engagements

initiatives.policy_implications: Establishes UWM as a national anchor for manufacturing AI and creates a repeatable industry-academic model for R1s in non-coastal innovation regions.

initiatives.title: Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat campus-wide rollout
initiatives.description: Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat is available to all UWM students, faculty, and staff through their UWM Microsoft 365 account with enterprise data protection (encryption, regular safety checks, contractually restricted from model training). UWM designates Copilot Chat as the only AI chatbot approved for use with University data, identifiable by the green-shield indicator in the UI.
initiatives.significance: Puts an enterprise-grade generative AI assistant in every UWM account with data-protection terms above the free tier, setting a campus-wide baseline for safe AI use.

investment.description: Included in UWM's Microsoft 365 enterprise agreement with additional legal and technical protections negotiated by UWM.

collaborations.description: Enterprise data protection agreement between UWM and Microsoft governs prompts/responses.

initiatives.policy_implications: Channels generative AI demand into a tool where data handling is auditable and contractually constrained, while shifting the institutional message from prohibition to approved-use.

initiatives.title: UWM AI Taskforce and GenAI Advisory Group
initiatives.description: Spring-2024 AI Taskforce co-led by CIO Scott Genung and Prof. Purush Papatla, comprising six work groups (Education & Curriculum, Student Experience, Research, Business Processes, Infrastructure and Data, Responsible AI) and ~50 stakeholders. Followed by an executive GenAI Advisory Group chaired by the CIO with Provost, SVC-Finance, and VC-Student-Affairs sponsorship. Deliverables include ~60 documented GenAI use cases, a three-tier tool portfolio (Equitable/Targeted/Special), a low/moderate/high data-risk framework, and pilot tool recommendations (Microsoft Copilot, ChatGPT for EDU, Ida, Maizy, Azure OpenAI).
initiatives.significance: A transparent, faculty-inclusive governance structure that produced usable artifacts (use-case inventory, data-risk framework, tool portfolio) rather than a vague policy statement.

investment.funding_source: Internal UWM IT and Academic Affairs
investment.description: Staff time and internal IT resources.

collaborations.description: Aligns with UW System Regent and UWSA policies; feeds systemwide AI news and initiatives hub.

initiatives.policy_implications: Demonstrates an operating cadence that converts policy abstractions into working tool portfolios and risk gates.

initiatives.title: CSI Factory GPT manufacturing chatbot
initiatives.description: A factory-specific generative pretrained transformer built on Microsoft Azure Machine Learning that lets shop-floor workers use natural language to query the UWM Connected Systems Institute testbed's operations and maintenance manuals for safety, quality, and troubleshooting. Co-created by UWM CS graduate student Shamar Webster with Microsoft CTO/AI leadership.
initiatives.significance: A concrete, student-built industrial AI deployment that shows how UWM's manufacturing AI research translates to tools usable by SMB manufacturers.

investment.funding_source: Microsoft gift to UWM Foundation for CSI (2024)
investment.description: Microsoft $1.2M gift to UWM Connected Systems Institute supported Factory GPT and related work, on top of prior Microsoft gifts totaling $2.7M.

collaborations.description: CSI industry consortium with Microsoft and Rockwell supporting student-led AI development.

tools_and_vendors.name: Azure Machine Learning
tools_and_vendors.role: Underlying ML platform for Factory GPT

initiatives.policy_implications: Exemplar of how R1 institutes can deliver production-adjacent AI prototypes through student-industry collaboration.

initiatives.title: Northwestern Mutual Data Science Institute (NMDSI) expansion and AI focus
initiatives.description: The NMDSI, co-founded in 2018 by Northwestern Mutual, Marquette University, and UWM, expanded in 2023 with a new $35M commitment (on top of nearly $40M prior), bringing cumulative commitments near $75M. In 2024 it added the Medical College of Wisconsin, Milwaukee School of Engineering, and Waukesha County Technical College, and focuses its next five years on AI; AI/data bias and ethics; behavioral economics; financial literacy; and health/wealth inequities. Funds endowed professorships, faculty grants, scholarships, curriculum, K-12 STEM/pre-college programs.
initiatives.significance: One of the largest corporate-academic AI partnerships in the Upper Midwest and UWM's deepest ongoing source of AI research, talent, and curriculum funding.

investment.description: Nearly $75M committed cumulatively since 2018, including a 2023 $35M renewal. Funds endowed professorships at each partner university, research grants, scholarships, curriculum, and pre-college programming.

collaborations.description: Six-institution consortium; recruits ~25% of Northwestern Mutual's employees and ~60% of its data workers.

initiatives.policy_implications: Models how a single corporate donor can scale data-science and AI capacity across a metropolitan higher-ed cluster; includes explicit ethics/bias and equity research lines.

initiatives.title: Milwaukee Five-University AI Pact
initiatives.description: On February 12, 2026 the chancellors and presidents of UWM, Marquette University, Medical College of Wisconsin, Milwaukee School of Engineering, and Waukesha County Technical College met at Northwestern Mutual's Milwaukee HQ to expand the existing NMDSI partnership and position Milwaukee as a national AI and data-science hub. Northwestern Mutual CEO Timothy Gerend announced the expansion; UWM Chancellor Thomas Gibson represented UWM.
initiatives.significance: Elevates AI positioning for the Milwaukee metro beyond a single institution into a cross-sector pact, with UWM as the regional R1 anchor.

investment.funding_source: Northwestern Mutual (annual research support) plus partner institutions
investment.description: The existing initiative raises ~$2M annually in university research funding, has collaborated with 160 faculty, launched 11 academic programs, and awarded $275K+ supporting 90 semester-long projects.

collaborations.description: Five higher-ed institutions plus Northwestern Mutual.

initiatives.policy_implications: Shows how a regional R1 can pool AI capacity with a research medical school, an engineering school, a private Catholic R1, and a technical college to cover the full workforce pipeline.

initiatives.title: Graduate Certificate in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning
initiatives.description: A 15-credit, five-course graduate certificate offered by UWM's Department of Computer Science (College of Engineering & Applied Science) and also through the Department of Electrical Engineering, with overlapping offerings via Universities of Wisconsin Online. Industry-advisory-shaped curriculum covers data science, AI, statistics, programming, plus electives in NLP, computer vision, and software engineering for AI/ML. Completable in one year, priced at roughly $14,000 online.
initiatives.significance: Provides a scalable, industry-aligned re-skill pathway for working professionals alongside UWM's traditional degree programs.

investment.description: Self-supporting certificate program with industry advisory board.

collaborations.description: Curriculum reviewed by industry partners to reflect employer demand.

initiatives.policy_implications: A template for R1 short-form AI credentials that preserve rigor while lowering the barrier for adult learners.

initiatives.title: MS in Data Science and Applied Artificial Intelligence
initiatives.description: UWM's interdisciplinary MS in Data Science and Applied Artificial Intelligence (offered through the Graduate School) is designed so graduates can practice analytics in any domain (healthcare, education, business, etc.). Coupled with a Data Science and Applied AI graduate certificate, a Lubar College MS in IT Management with an AI and Data Analytics concentration, and a Lubar business analytics certificate.
initiatives.significance: Broadest graduate-level AI pipeline in metro Milwaukee, explicitly designed to place data scientists into domain-specific roles.

investment.description: NMDSI funds an endowed professorship and curriculum expansion that feeds this program.

collaborations.description: NMDSI supports curriculum development and faculty hires for data science programming.

initiatives.policy_implications: Domain-embedded data science degree model as an alternative to a stand-alone AI degree.

initiatives.title: School of Continuing Education AI courses
initiatives.description: UWM's School of Continuing Education offers online AI courses for working professionals covering prompt engineering, ethical AI, deep learning fundamentals, and integrating AI in the workplace. Positioned as a non-degree upskilling track complementing the graduate certificates and degree programs.
initiatives.significance: Extends UWM's AI learning pipeline into continuing-education adults and employer-sponsored cohorts, critical for regional workforce readiness.

investment.funding_source: UWM SCE tuition and employer-sponsored enrollment

collaborations.description: Courses designed for professional upskilling in Wisconsin employers.

initiatives.policy_implications: Companion pathway that operationalizes the AI Taskforce's call for broad AI literacy beyond matriculated students.

initiatives.title: CASL (formerly CETL) AI and Teaching support
initiatives.description: UWM's Center for Advancing Student Learning (CASL), formerly the Center for Excellence in Teaching and Learning (CETL), is the primary faculty-development hub for AI. Maintains an 'AI and Teaching' knowledge base, sample syllabus language, assignment-design checklists, FERPA-aware tool advisories, and monthly Active Teaching Labs. Hosts AI town halls and workshops tied to the AI Taskforce deliverables.
initiatives.significance: Moves faculty AI preparedness from ad hoc department guidance to a coordinated institution-level support channel.

collaborations.description: Shares AI-in-teaching practice across UW System teaching centers.

initiatives.policy_implications: Mature model for R1s whose policy is 'encouraged' rather than 'required': central T&L unit absorbs the coordination load.

initiatives.title: Pounce student-services chatbot
initiatives.description: 'Ask Pounce!' is an AI chatbot embedded in the OneStop website that provides 24/7 responses on enrollment, academic records, billing, and financial aid questions for UWM students. Branded with UWM's panther mascot.
initiatives.significance: Represents the baseline of AI-driven student support automation at a public R1 -- always-on answers for transactional student questions.

investment.funding_source: UWM Student Financial Services / OneStop operating budget
investment.description: Vendor-hosted chatbot; specific vendor not publicly disclosed.

collaborations.description: Vendor-delivered service in the OneStop web surface.

initiatives.policy_implications: Illustrates typical public-R1 deployment of chatbots for financial aid and enrollment questions.

initiatives.title: NSF Campus Cyberinfrastructure grant for Mortimer HPC cluster upgrade
initiatives.description: $400,000 NSF Campus Cyberinfrastructure award (2021) led by Prof. Philip Chang (Physics) with 12 co-PIs across physics, engineering, and freshwater sciences, used to upgrade UWM's aging supercomputing cluster and expand participation in the Open Science Grid. The cluster (now branded 'Mortimer') processes 600,000+ jobs/year and supports seven of UWM's top 10 research projects. Current hardware: 3,632 CPU cores, 4 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, plus high-memory AMD nodes up to 1TB RAM.
initiatives.significance: Represents UWM's pre-existing computational foundation that underpins AI research, but is modest compared to AI-era R1 GPU investments.

investment.funding_source: National Science Foundation, Campus Cyberinfrastructure program
investment.description: $400K NSF CC* award plus internal matching.

collaborations.description: NSF-funded upgrade tied to participation in distributed national computing consortium.

initiatives.policy_implications: Highlights the constrained GPU footprint available at regional R1s relative to top-tier AI-era computing investments; further expansion likely required as AI workloads grow.

research_centers.name: Connected Systems Institute (CSI)
research_centers.focus: Industry 4.0, smart manufacturing, applied AI, IoT. Hosts Microsoft's first manufacturing-focused AI Co-Innovation Lab and developed Factory GPT.

research_centers.name: Northwestern Mutual Data Science Institute (NMDSI)
research_centers.focus: Data science, AI, AI/data bias and ethics, behavioral economics, financial literacy, and health/wealth inequities. Six-institution consortium anchored at UWM and Marquette.

Graduate Certificate in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (Electrical Engineering)
Graduate Certificate in Data Science and Applied Artificial Intelligence
MS in Data Science and Applied Artificial Intelligence
MS Information Technology Management: AI and Data Analytics concentration
Undergraduate Certificate in Artificial Intelligence & Analytics for Business

School of Continuing Education Artificial Intelligence courses (prompt engineering, ethical AI, deep learning, AI in the workplace)

enterprise_platforms.product: Azure AI / Azure OpenAI (pilot tool portfolio)

computing_infrastructure.summary: UWM operates the Mortimer Faculty Research Cluster (3,632 CPU cores, 4 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, AMD nodes up to 1TB RAM) and the Peregrine educational cluster (232 cores). The cluster processes ~600,000 jobs/year and participates in the Open Science Grid. Expanded via a 2021 $400K NSF Campus Cyberinfrastructure award led by Philip Chang. GPU capacity is modest relative to leading AI-era R1s.

investments_and_gifts.title: Microsoft AI infrastructure and Co-Innovation Lab commitment (Wisconsin)
investments_and_gifts.purpose: Southeastern Wisconsin AI infrastructure and digital transformation, which includes the Microsoft AI Co-Innovation Lab at UWM CSI.

investments_and_gifts.title: Northwestern Mutual Data Science Institute renewal
investments_and_gifts.purpose: Five-year renewal of NMDSI with Marquette and UWM, extending funding for AI, ethics, health/wealth equity research through 2028.

investments_and_gifts.title: Microsoft gift to UWM Connected Systems Institute
investments_and_gifts.purpose: Supports CSI research, Factory GPT, and manufacturing AI workforce development (third Microsoft gift to CSI, bringing total to roughly $2.7M).

investments_and_gifts.title: NSF Campus Cyberinfrastructure grant for HPC upgrade
investments_and_gifts.purpose: Expand UWM supercomputing cluster and Open Science Grid participation to support AI, physics, astrophysics, molecular modeling research.

industry_and_research_partnerships.purpose: AI Co-Innovation Lab, Copilot enterprise deployment, CSI Factory GPT, and broader AI infrastructure partnership.
NMDSI partnership covering AI, data science, ethics, behavioral economics, and workforce talent.

industry_and_research_partnerships.partner: Marquette University; Medical College of Wisconsin; Milwaukee School of Engineering; Waukesha County Technical College
industry_and_research_partnerships.purpose: NMDSI six-institution consortium and February 2026 Milwaukee AI pact.
Industrial automation partner supporting CSI manufacturing AI testbed.

industry_and_research_partnerships.purpose: Co-anchors for the Microsoft AI Co-Innovation Lab at UWM CSI.
HPC upgrade grant and Open Science Grid participation.

student_facing_ai.name: Ask Pounce!
student_facing_ai.scope: 24/7 student-services chatbot on UWM OneStop site; covers enrollment, academic records, billing, and financial aid.

student_facing_ai.name: Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat (student access)
student_facing_ai.scope: Available to all enrolled UWM students via their Microsoft 365 account with enterprise data protection.

student_facing_ai.name: UWM Libraries Generative AI and Research guide
student_facing_ai.scope: Libraries-curated guide on using generative AI for research assignments, citation, and integrity.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: NMDSI research lines on AI/data bias and ethics and on health/wealth inequities
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Two of the five focus areas for the 2023-2028 NMDSI renewal explicitly target AI and data bias and ethics and health/wealth inequities, with funding for research grants, faculty, and K-12 pre-college programming aimed at underrepresented students.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Three-tier AI tool portfolio (Equitable/Targeted/Special)
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: AI Taskforce recommended stratifying AI tooling so all students/faculty/staff have access to a baseline enterprise tool (Copilot Chat) regardless of college or role, reducing the intra-campus AI access gap.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: AI detector discouragement policy to protect FERPA and reduce bias impacts on students
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: UWM explicitly discourages AI-detection tools because of FERPA risks and documented false-positive bias against non-native English speakers and students from underrepresented groups.

regulatory_context.summary: Wisconsin has no comprehensive statewide AI higher-education policy. Governor Evers created a Governor's Task Force on Workforce and AI via Executive Order #211 (August 2023) and signed 2025 Wisconsin Act 34 criminalizing coercive deepfakes, but classroom/AI policy is largely set at the system and institutional level. UWM operates under the Universities of Wisconsin (UW System) policy stack: Regent Policy Documents 25-3 (Acceptable Use) and 25-5 (Information Security), plus UWSA policies 1031 (Data Classification), 1039 (Risk Management), and 1040 (Privacy). UW System President Jay Rothman has articulated a systemwide AI strategy treating the 13 universities as a single 'AI-enabled ecosystem,' with notable peer activity including UW-Madison's new College of Computing and AI (approved December 2025, operational July 2026), UW-Eau Claire's 2025 AI majors, and UW-Stout's 360-degree AI-in-curriculum approach.

narrative_summary: UWM's AI posture is pragmatic and partnership-driven rather than flagship-strategy-driven. Under new Chancellor Thomas Gibson (July 2025) and CIO Scott Genung (since 2023), the university has not published a single named 'UWM AI Strategy' document, but has stood up a robust two-body governance model: a broad spring-2024 AI Taskforce of ~50 stakeholders across six work groups that delivered an inventory of ~60 use cases, a three-tier tool portfolio, and a low/moderate/high data-risk framework; and a smaller executive GenAI Advisory Group chaired by the CIO with Provost, SVC-Finance, and VC-Student-Affairs sponsorship. Policy is layered on top of UW System Regent and UWSA rules (RPD 25-3, 25-5; UWSA 1031, 1039, 1040) rather than a bespoke UWM policy -- a cautious stance that encourages (but does not require) syllabus-level AI statements, discourages AI detection tools on FERPA grounds, and routes all University-data AI use through Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat enterprise, which rolled out campus-wide to all students, faculty, and staff in April 2026. UWM's distinctive strengths are in applied AI research and workforce: the Connected Systems Institute anchors Microsoft's first manufacturing-focused AI Co-Innovation Lab (launched June 2025 as part of Microsoft's $3.3B Wisconsin investment), hosts the student-built Factory GPT manufacturing chatbot, and has attracted cumulative Microsoft gifts of ~$2.7M. The Northwestern Mutual Data Science Institute, co-founded with Marquette in 2018 and expanded in 2023 with a $35M renewal (~$75M cumulative) and again in 2024 to include MCW, MSOE, and WCTC, funds endowed professorships, AI/ethics research, and curriculum across six institutions. This fed directly into a February 2026 five-university Milwaukee AI pact at Northwestern Mutual HQ pushing the region as a national AI hub. Academic offerings include graduate certificates in AI/ML from both CS and Electrical Engineering, an MS in Data Science and Applied AI, a Lubar ITM concentration in AI and Data Analytics, an undergraduate AI & Analytics for Business certificate, and a growing School of Continuing Education AI catalog. Known gaps: UWM has a modest GPU footprint (4 NVIDIA A100s on the Mortimer cluster), no named Chief AI Officer, no required AI literacy general education requirement, and no large public-facing AI-strategy document, leaving the institution reliant on taskforce artifacts and UW System policy scaffolding. Its CASL (formerly CETL) teaching center provides the main faculty-development surface for AI pedagogy and assessment design.

Exact user counts for Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat, Pounce chatbot vendor identity, and user adoption metrics are not publicly disclosed.

Pounce chatbot launch date and vendor not disclosed on public pages.
